articles.title: Finland issues wanted notice for hacker behind massive psychotherapy data breach
articles.summary: The defendant's lawyer told Finnish media that he does not know where his client is but believes Kivimäki is outside Finland.

articles.title: Researchers Say Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Rogue Extensions Trigger Gmail Reads
articles.summary: Any other browser extension that can run a script on claude.ai can still trigger Claude for Chrome tasks aimed at your Gmail, your latest Google Doc and its comments, and your Calendar. articles.title: Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: The ColdFusion security defects could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code or elevate their privileges. The post Adobe Patches Critical ColdFusion Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments. articles.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
articles.summary: Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch the flaw. [...]

articles.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
articles.summary: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]

articles.title: You Don't Have to Run an Exploit to Know If You're Vulnerable
articles.summary: Many vulnerabilities cannot be safely validated with live exploits, either because no exploit exists or the affected systems are too critical to test. articles.title: 7 Severe Vulnerabilities Patched in VMware Avi Load Balancer
articles.summary: The flaws can be exploited for authentication bypass, remote code execution, privilege escalation, and directory traversal. The post 7 Severe Vulnerabilities Patched in VMware Avi Load Balancer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: NATO logistics, Ukrainian troops are top subjects of Russian camera hacks, advisory says
articles.summary: Dutch intelligence officials report that at least one Russian agency is compromising internet-connected cameras across Europe to spy on military logistics and Ukrainian personnel.

articles.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two access control-related flaws impacting the RabbitMQ message broker service that could allow attackers to leak OAuth client secrets, expose enterprise messaging infrastructure to takeover risks, and bypass tenant boundaries. articles.title: Unpatched Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Extensions Read Gmail, Calendar
articles.summary: A ClaudeBleed-linked vulnerability reportedly persists across eight patches, exposing potentially sensitive data to other extensions. The post Unpatched Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Extensions Read Gmail, Calendar appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts, evade MFA
articles.summary: Two new phishing kits, Jalisco and OmegaLord, have been discovered in attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts, using techniques that defeat multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]

articles.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the modern firmware standard. articles.title: How Pentera Turns AI Security Workflows into Validation Engines
articles.summary: AI security agents are starting to influence real security decisions. They summarize findings, prioritize remediation, recommend next steps, and help teams move faster. articles.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
articles.summary: At least two distinct threat actors are weaponizing a novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing in cloud campaigns, while slipping past telemetry. articles.title: SAP Patches Critical Vulnerabilities in NetWeaver, Approuter, Commerce Cloud

articles.title: Vulnerability in FIFA’s Network
articles.summary: FIFA’s network was vulnerable to anyone with even minimal access.

articles.title: US, Allies Warn of Russian Cyberattacks Targeting Critical Infrastructure Routers
articles.summary: Multiple state-sponsored APTs are compromising poorly secured devices across critical infrastructure sector networks. The post US, Allies Warn of Russian Cyberattacks Targeting Critical Infrastructure Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: US sanctions VPN, malware providers for enabling ransomware attacks
articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned two individuals and one entity for enabling ransomware attacks against U.S. organizations. [...]

articles.title: Multiple Jscrambler Packages Impacted by Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: A threat actor poisoned several Jscrambler NPM package versions to drop a cross-platform credential stealer. The post Multiple Jscrambler Packages Impacted by Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed. articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including ransomware attacks against Americans. articles.summary: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog. articles.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
articles.summary: Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform. articles.title: Hackers steal Lidl customer data from external service provider
articles.summary: The retailer said the incident did not affect its online shopping platform itself but involved a separately stored customer database maintained by a third-party provider. According to notifications sent to Lidl's German, Belgian and Dutch customers on Friday, the attackers briefly accessed the file

articles.title: Japan's largest taxi operator shuts systems after cyberattack
articles.summary: Japan's largest taxi operator, Nihon Kotsu, announced that its systems were compromised in a cyberattack, forcing the company to shut down part of its infrastructure. [...]

articles.title: Hackers backdoor Jscrambler npm package with infostealer malware
articles.summary: The Jscrambler client-side web security company disclosed that a threat actor published a malicious version of its npm package that has been downloaded almost 1,500 times. [...]

articles.title: New CrashStealer malware poses as Apple crash reporting tool
articles.summary: A new macOS information-stealing malware called CrashStealer pretends to be Apple's crash-reporting tool to steal credentials, keychain data, and crypto wallets. [...]

articles.title: VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions against First VPN Service (1VPNS) and its Ukrainian administrator for aiding ransomware groups. Separately, a Belarusian man was sanctioned for malware "cryptors."

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems. articles.title: Russian celebrity journalist Ksenia Sobchak says hackers accessed Telegram channels via email breach
articles.summary: Following the breach of several of her Telegram channels, controversial Russian journalist Ksenia Sobchak claimed published screenshots of her correspondence with political figures were fake.

articles.title: CISA warns of actively exploited RCE flaws in Joomla extensions
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in the iCagenda and Balbooa Forms extensions for Joomla to achieve remote code execution through arbitrary file uploads. [...]

articles.title: Lidl discloses online shop breach after service provider hack

articles.summary: Give an AI assistant a memory and access to your inbox, and you hand an attacker a way to rewrite what it thinks it knows about you. articles.title: RabbitMQ Vulnerability Threatens Enterprise Systems
articles.summary: Unauthenticated attackers could obtain the broker's confidential OAuth client secret, allowing them to take control of the broker. The post RabbitMQ Vulnerability Threatens Enterprise Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: EU sanctions Russian GRU military hackers over cyberattacks
articles.summary: The European Union and the United Kingdom jointly sanctioned dozens of Russian individuals and entities and accused Russia of coordinating a network of hacking groups responsible for attacks across Europe. [...]

articles.title: Zimbra Patches Critical Code Execution Vulnerability
articles.summary: The flaw results in malicious code embedded in crafted emails being executed when the emails are opened. The post Zimbra Patches Critical Code Execution Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Russia's FSB blamed for Poland grid attack as UK and EU impose first joint cyber sanctions
articles.summary: The allies blamed Center 16, the FSB’s signals intelligence arm, for acts of attempted cyber sabotage targeting Poland’s energy sector and water treatment facilities, alongside “a wide range of malicious cyber activities with growing severity.”
